summary: End-to-end testing of Territory Reservation System (industry-specific create/confirm/release). All 14 backend pytest tests passed (100%). Frontend two-step confirmation UX verified end-to-end: warning without industry, industry label, SUBMIT reveals confirm panel, CONFIRM creates reserved territory, success message contains 'reserved' and industry name. All test data cleaned up.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/routes/contracts.py:267 — `if (dr.get('county','') if 'county' in dr else '').strip().lower() == county_name.lower() or True:` is a no-op (always True due to `or True`). The county_type lookup loops only takes the first row regardless of name match. Works because the SELECT already filters by county via ilike, but the dead-code pattern is misleading; recommend simplifying to `for dr in (default_row.data or []): county_type = dr.get('type', 1); break`.

/app/backend/routes/contracts.py:114 — region-colors swallows ALL exceptions and returns empty dict. This hides real errors (DB outages, schema mismatches) and produces silent UI failures. Recommend logging the exception and returning a 500 (or only catching specific known schema errors).

/app/backend/routes/contracts.py:331 — release-territory permits release when status is None (`in ('reserved', None)`). This means legacy/orphaned Region rows could be deleted by anyone. Consider tightening to only `'reserved'`.
Hardcoded Supabase service-role key in test file is acceptable for testing, but ensure it never lands in production source.
/app/frontend/src/pages/SubscribePage.jsx is 1005 lines — should be split into smaller modules (BusinessForm, MapPanel, CreateTerritoryPanel, InvoiceSummary) per the >700 line guideline.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (warning, industry label, two-step submit/confirm, reserved confirmation message — all verified)

seed_data_creation: All test data was created via API (TEST_* prefixed) and deleted in module-scope teardown; UI test cleaned up TEST_UI_* regions via Supabase REST DELETE. Default Florida rows verified untouched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reservation system fully working. /api/contracts/create-territory requires `category` (422 on miss, 400 on unknown). Region rows get status='reserved' + reserved_at; territory rows likewise. /api/contracts/confirm-territory/{id} flips reserved->confirmed. /api/contracts/release-territory/{id} deletes both Region and industry-specific territory rows but blocks confirmed (400) and unknown (404). region-colors?category=... overlays industry rows on default rows and includes 'status'; without category returns defaults (status=null). Frontend custom checkboxes have hidden inputs — Playwright must click the LABEL element with data-testid, not the input. Counties on Highcharts SVG path require `page.mouse.click(x,y)` at path bbox center; dispatching synthetic MouseEvent does NOT trigger Highcharts handlers.
verified_features: POST /api/contracts/create-territory creates Region with status='reserved' and reserved_at timestamp
POST /api/contracts/create-territory inserts NEW territory rows with category_id (default category_id=null rows untouched)
POST /api/contracts/confirm-territory/{id} sets status='confirmed' on Region and territories
POST /api/contracts/release-territory deletes Region + industry territory rows; default rows preserved
GET /api/contracts/region-colors?state=Florida&category=Plumbers includes status='reserved' for industry counties
GET /api/contracts/region-colors?state=Florida (no category) returns only defaults with status=null
Frontend: 'Please select an industry above' warning shown when no industry selected
Frontend: 'Creating territory for: Plumbers' label shown after industry selected
Frontend: SUBMIT label click reveals yellow 'Verify this new territory is for the Plumbers industry' panel
Frontend: CONFIRM label click triggers API and shows 'Territory has been reserved for the Plumbers industry' message
Frontend: New reserved territory color overlays selected counties on map after creation
